name: BS 7671 Table 54.7 — Adiabatic CPC Sizing
implementation_note: Adiabatic equation S = sqrt(I² × t) / k. Requires lookup against the k-factor table (Table 54.7) keyed on (conductor material, insulation, conductor temp limit). Also requires reading the protective device's I²t let-through curve at the prospective fault current. Inline LLM math reliably mis-reads the k table and miscalls the let-through interpolation; must be tool-executed.

inputs.description: Prospective earth-fault current at the point of consideration (typically from PSCC at the load end)
Time for the protective device to clear the fault at fault_current_a (read from its time-current curve)
Cross-sectional area of the line conductor
CPC material; usually matches phase conductor
CPC insulation class — drives the k table column
Conductor temperature before fault (usually ambient or operating)
true if the cable's armour/sheath/conduit is used as the CPC — drives different k lookup

required_csa_mm2.description: Minimum CPC cross-section satisfying the adiabatic equation

adiabatic_pass.description: true if a standard CPC size meets the requirement; false if special sizing needed

table_54_3_minimum.description: Cross-reference: Table 54.3 minimum vs phase csa (the lazy-engineer fallback)

runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { required_csa_mm2, k_factor_used, adiabatic_pass, selected_standard_csa, table_54_3_minimum }, error?: string }
